notes: Rotterdam Study 2004 — 4807 adults, 10yr follow-up; highest K2 tertile had 57% lower CV mortality and 41% lower CHD risk vs lowest; aortic calcification reduced; K1 showed no association; landmark observational evidence

2021 narrative review — Vitamin K2 neglected player in cardiovascular health; MGP mechanism for arterial calcification prevention; arterial stiffness RCTs; acknowledges need for larger RCTs; credible journal (Open Heart / BMJ sub-journal)

2022 systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 RCTs, 6425 participants — K2 maintains/improves lumbar BMD when combined with D3 or calcium; fracture reduction evidence mixed; MK-4 and MK-7 shown most effective for bone
